title: Create a view based on search results
excerpt:
I have a board with lots of groups. Some groups have the phrase “[completed]” in the title. I want to create a view that shows all groups except [completed] and I don’t want to have to do it manually each time. 
Is there…

posts.author: bhamiltonedu (Belinda Hamilton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board with lots of groups. Some groups have the phrase “[completed]” in the title. I want to create a view that shows all groups except [completed] and I don’t want to have to do it manually each time.

Is there a way to create the kind of view automatically using standard Monday.com features? I thought search results might work but I haven’t found a way to save a search as a view everyone can use.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi@bhamiltonedu ,
Unfortunately, there isn’t a built-in feature in monday.com to automatically create a view that excludes groups containing the phrase “[completed]” in their title. While you can use search results to temporarily filter out these groups, saving this search as a view that everyone can use is not possible.
